There are four types of candidates in an institute preparing for the UPSC. The number of students of Engineering, Science, Commerce and Humanities is 40, 60, 50 and 30 respectively and the respective percentage of students who qualified the UPSC is 80%, 75%, 60% and 50% respectively. The overall percentage of successful candidates in the institute is :

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)

Q2 Not checked
General Aptitude • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +1 / -0
Not attempted

The number 12375 is divisible by

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(a, b, c)
• For a number to be divisible by 3 and 9, the sum of its digits should be divisible by 3 and 9
respectively. Here, 1 + 2 + 3 + 7 + 5 = 18, which is divisible 3 and 9 both. Hence, the number is
divisible by both 3 and 9.
• For a number to be divisible by 11, the difference between the sum of digits in the odd
positions and even positions must be a multiple of 11 or 0. Here, (5 + 3 + 1) – (7 + 2) = 0. Hence,
the number is divisible by 11.

Q5 Not checked
General Aptitude • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Sum of all numbers n such that n! ends with 30 zeros.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
124! ends with 28 zeros and 125! ends with 31 zeros.
Therefore, there is no integer n with n! ending in exactly 30 zeros.

Q9 Not checked
General Aptitude • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Read the passage carefully and choose the best answer to the following question from the four alternatives given below.
Among the natural resources which can be called upon in national plan for development, possibly
the most important is human labour; without productive labour force, including effective leadership
and intelligent middle management, no amount of foreign assistance or natural wealth can ensure
successful development and modernisation. One essential factor is usually overlooked or ignored.
The forgotten factor is the role of women. Development will be handicapped as long as women
remain second-class citizens, uneducated, without any voice in family or community decisions,
without legal or economic status, married when they are still practically children, and thenceforth
producing one baby after another, often only to see half of them die before they are of school age.
We can enhance development by improving ‘woman power’ by giving women opportunity to develop themselves.
The most important natural resource for national development is

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
The passage clearly states that among natural resources, human labour is the most important for national development.

Q10 Not checked
General Aptitude • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

In the following question, a part of the sentence is given in bold. Choose the correct alternative that best improves the bold part. If no improvement is needed, select “No improvement.”
He likes to drive his car at a speed of eighty kilometres each hour.

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
The correct expression is “eighty kilometres an hour” (or per hour). The word “each” is
grammatically incorrect in this context. “Correct sentence: “He likes to drive his car at a speed of
eighty kilometres an hour.”

NAT
Correct answer: 9.5
Solution

Q17 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are correct regarding quicksand condition?

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(a, c)
Quicksand is simply sand saturated with water, which reduces friction between the sand particles
and makes the mixture behave like a fluid when disturbed. It can also be seen as a viscous fluid
with a unit weight twice that of water. Hence, any person can easily float in it, material being
viscous renders movement of body very tiring. But there is no development of negative pressure
or ‘sucking’ action in this phenomenon. In very pervious sands and gravels, the large discharge
required to maintain a quick condition make it improbable for its occurrence.

Q18 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements regarding the versenate method for water hardness determination is/are correct?

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(a, b, d)
Hardness can be measured directly by titration with ethylenediamine tetraacetic acid (EDTA)
using eriochrome black T (EBT) as an indicator. The EBT reacts with the divalent metallic cations,
forming a complex that is red in color. The EDTA replaces the EBT in the complex, and when the
replacement is complete, the solution changes its colour from red to blue. If 0.01 M EDTA is used,
1.0 mL of the titrant measures 1.0 mg of hardness as CaCO3.
